verif_result.fix_hint:
Deterministic structural preflight rejected the RTL before iverilog/Verilator.
Violated rule(s): window_not_registered, readmemh_missing, readmemh_missing, output_counter_missing, coord_scheduler_missing.
Repair the indicted construct exactly; do not touch unrelated logic.
Violations:
- [window_not_registered] Spatial conv must either instantiate line_buf_window or declare a shift-register window as 'reg [...] window [...]'. No such reg was found — a wire/assign 'window' is rebuilt combinationally every cycle, which blows up synth cones and loses the sliding-window invariant.
- [readmemh_missing] Weights must be loaded via $readmemh("<weights_path>", weights) or $readmemh("<bank_path>", weights_bank<N>) inside an initial block, OR the top-level must instantiate conv_datapath with WEIGHTS_PATH/BIAS_PATH parameters. None was found.
- [readmemh_missing] Biases must be loaded via $readmemh("<bias_path>", biases) or via conv_datapath's BIAS_PATH parameter. Neither was found.
- [output_counter_missing] Spatial conv / maxpool must bound its output count with either an `outputs_emitted` reg (or `out_row` / `out_col`) or a coord_scheduler instantiation. Without a bounded counter the FSM has no frame-level stop condition and Verilator can hang on partial valid_out firings.
- [coord_scheduler_missing] Spatial conv / maxpool modules must instantiate coord_scheduler from rtl_library/coord_scheduler.v. No coord_scheduler reference was found in the RTL. Rolling your own coordinate/wrap/stride/termination logic is the historically most bug-prone piece of the pipeline — do not reinvent it.

verif_result.classifier_reason: Structural preflight caught five missing RTL constructs (window_not_registered, readmemh_missing x2, output_counter_missing, coord_scheduler_missing) on the first Foundry attempt. All violations are fixable RTL omissions — no resource overflow, no bus-width violation, no toolchain failure. The depthwise-conv contract is viable at 768-bit bus on xczu9eg; Foundry simply omitted required library instantiations and register declarations. A targeted repair pass should resolve all five violations.
